data.title: Mid to low cap consumer staples stocks with the strongest earnings momentum
data.description:
As the earnings season kicks off, we look at mid-to-low market cap consumer staples stocks with strong earnings momentum, which often attract investor attention as positive analyst revisions to earnings estimates can signal improving fundamentals.

The EPS Revision rating evaluates how analysts have adjusted their earnings per share estimates for a company over recent periods, with upgrades suggesting growing confidence in a company’s profitability outlook.

The following low to mid cap consumer staples sector stocks have earned the highest possible EPS revision rating of A+, indicating the most favorable trajectory of analyst earnings estimate changes.

The following consumer staples stocks currently hold an A+ EPS Revision Grade:

  1. The Andersons (ANDE) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  2. Beyond Meat (BYND) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  3. Freshpet (FRPT) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  4. John B. Sanfilippo & Son (JBSS) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  5. Oddity Tech (ODD) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  6. Reynolds Consumer Products (REYN) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  7. Turning Point Brands (TPB) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  8. Village Farms International (VFF) — EPS Revision Grade: A+

Consumer Staples ETFs: (XLP), (VDC), (IYK), (FSTA), (KXI), and (RSPS)

data.title: Columbus McKinnon expects Q3 net sales between $250M and $260M
data.description:
• Columbus McKinnon Corporation (CMCO) announced on Wednesday that the company currently expects Q3 net sales to range between $250M and $260M  versus a consensus of $241.60M and between $747M and $757M for the nine months ended December 31, 2025.
  • The company currently expects Q3 adjusted EPS to range between $0.58 and $0.63 versus a consensus of $0.57 and between $1.70 and $1.75 for the nine months ended December 31, 2025.
  • The company currently expects Q3 adjusted EBITDA to range between $38M and $40M and between $115M and $117M for the nine months ended December 31, 2025.
  • In addition, the company estimates that orders received during Q3 will range between $245M and $250M.
  • The company estimates that backlog will range between $335 million and $345M as of December 31, 2025, down 3% at the midpoint from a backlog of $351.6M in the second quarter of fiscal 2026 and up 5% at the midpoint from a backlog of $322.5M at the end of fiscal 2025.
  • CMCO is +0.92% to $20.215.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Finland plans to buy anti-personnel landmines, train staff on deployment
data.description:
Finland’s armed forces on Wednesday said they will begin procuring anti-personnel landmines and training soldiers, conscripts and reservists in their use later this year, following the country’s formal exit from the Ottawa Convention banning such weapons, which took effect on January 10.

Finland joined several EU and NATO countries bordering Russia including Lithuania, Latvia, Estonia and Poland in withdrawing from the treaty amid heightened security concerns linked to Russia.

The Defense Forces said discussions with domestic defense companies and training programs would begin in early 2026, with the aim of having newly developed mines and related training equipment available by 2027.

The military said the weapons would be developed in cooperation with Finland’s defense industry and produced domestically. Officials stressed that the mines would not be deployed during peacetime and would only be used in emergency situations.

data.title: Wall Street opens in the red as traders digest PPI retail sales reports
data.description:
U.S. stock indexes opened lower on Wednesday as the wholesale inflation report came in as expected, while retail sales came in hotter. 

The S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.3%, while the Dow (DJI) was -0.1%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND), -0.5%.

Treasury yields moved lower across the curve. The U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) fell to 3.53%, down 0.34%. The benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) declined to 4.16%, losing 0.48%. The 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) eased to 4.82%, down 0.41%.

Producer price growth came in a little hotter in November, as expected. The current account deficit narrowed more than expected in Q3 2025.

Retail sales jumped past consensus in November. 

Existing home sales and business inventories will come in during market hours. There are also a few Federal Reserve speakers lined up.

data.title: Canadian Natural Resources poised to acquire Tourmaline’s natural gas business - Globe & Mail
data.description:
Canadian Natural Resources (CNQ) is in talks to buy a $1B-plus portfolio of natural gas properties in Alberta from Tourmaline Oil (TRMLF), the Globe and Mail reported Wednesday.

Canada's biggest shale producer filed the paperwork for federal approval of a transaction involving Tourmaline (TRMLF) with Canada's Competition Bureau on December 30, but details of the potential deal had not been disclosed publicly.

Canadian Natural (CNQ) is seeking preliminary regulatory feedback on a potential acquisition of assets in the Montney Basin located in Alberta's Peace River region that Tourmaline (TRMLF) put up for sale in November prior to announcing a purchase, according to the report.

Tourmaline (TRMLF) is one of the biggest natural gas producers in Canada's Montney Basin shale play, a region that produces ~10B cf/day of natural gas, roughly half of Canada's total output; the company's Peace River operations include 2,428 horizontal wells, 34 gas plants and 15.5K km of pipelines.

Interest in the Montney Basin has increased because of last year's launch of the LNG Canada export terminal in British Columbia, and the basin provides some of the most economic production in Canada.

data.title: Apple, Qualcomm face supply woes over Japan's glass cloth: report
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) and Qualcomm (QCOM) are concerned about the strained supplies of Japan's high-end glass cloth fiber, Nikkei Asia reported.

Glass cloth is a vital part in chip substrates and printed circuit boards, or PCBs, which themselves are the building blocks of electronic devices, and the most advanced types of this cloth are made almost exclusively by one Japanese firm, Nitto Boseki, or Nittobo for short, the report added.

Apple and Qualcomm did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Apple was an early adopter of Nittobo's glass cloth for iPhones. Initially, there was little issue with supply. The AI boom has increased the demand for high-performance PCBs manufactured with premium glass cloth, meaning that even AI companies now want Nittobo's products, putting not just Apple but also mobile chip maker Qualcomm (QCOM) at risk of supply shortages, the report noted.

Short supplies of the glass cloth fiber have Apple scrambling against the likes of Nvidia (NVDA), Alphabet's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Google and Amazon (AMZN) to secure the same scarce resource, the report added.

The short supply of glass fiber and the resulting crunch in PCB supplies are creating what one industry insider calls "one of the biggest bottlenecks for the electronics-making and AI industry for 2026," according to the report.

Amid growing concerns, Apple dispatched staff to Japan last autumn, and stationing them at Mitsubishi Gas Chemical, or MGC, to try to secure more supplies of materials used in bismaleimide triazine, or BT, substrates, to report added.

Several types of chips require BT substrates as their base, including those used in iPhones and other mobile devices. And to make its BT substrate materials, MGC requires Nittobo's glass cloth.

Apple even asked the Japanese government officials if they could help secure more supplies from Nittobo to meet its 2026 product roadmap, the report noted.

MGC told the news outlet that the company's "related business division is closely discussing with important customers including direct and indirect ones to search out the solution of current materials supply issues," but declined to comment further.

Earlier, Nikkei had reported that Nvidia and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) also sent staff to visit Nittobo in hopes of getting supplies needed for AI chips, the report noted.

Nvidia and AMD did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

"No additional capacity is no additional capacity, even if you pressure Nittobo," said an executive with a substrate supplier serving AMD, Nvidia and Apple, the report noted. "The way we see it, the situation will only truly improve meaningfully when Nittobo's new capacity goes online in the second half of 2027."

Apple is also working to find alternative sources, including sending staff to a small Chinese glass fiber maker known as Grace Fabric Technology, or GFT, and asking MGC to help oversee the Chinese material supplier's quality improvement, the report noted.

Meanwhile, Qualcomm has visited another, smaller Japanese glass cloth supplier, Unitika, to see if it could help ease the supply. However, the quantity that Unitika makes is far less than that of Nittobo, as per the report.

"This is one of the biggest bottlenecks for the electronics-making and AI industry for 2026," an executive from a supplier to Nvidia and Apple told the news outlet.

The special type of glass they are seeking is formally known as low-coefficient-of-thermal-expansion, or CTE, glass but is commonly called T-glass. The product is valued for its dimensional stability, rigidity and ability to facilitate high-speed data transmission, which is vital in AI computing and other premium processor chips, the report noted.

Many new entrants are hoping to capitalize on the constrained supply, such as Taiwan Glass, a traditional glass manufacturer based in Taipei, and China's Taishan Fiberglass, Grace Fabric and Kingboard Laminates Group, as per the report.

However, the technological barriers to entry are high — every glass fiber is thinner than a human hair and must be perfectly round and free of any bubbles — and new entrants are struggling to achieve adequate capacity and consistent quality, the report noted. No tech giant is willing to risk mounting their high-end chips on substrates which could compromise the quality of their final products, industry executives told the news outlet.

"The stability of T-glass cloth is a decisive factor for substrate quality," said a source with a substrate equipment maker, while a PCB making executive noted that the product's location deep inside a device's substrate means it cannot be taken out and fixed later, the report added.

Apple pioneered the use of high-end glass fiber in its smartphones, well before AI chip leaders started deploying such materials at scale in their products. However, even Apple has been caught off guard by buyers like Nvidia, Amazon and Google entering the fray, the report noted.

Apple has talked to suppliers about using less-advanced types of glass cloth, but it would take time to test and verify alternative materials, doing little to immediately improve the situation, the report added.

T-glass is not the only product facing issues in the tech supply chain. AI-driven investments have already impacted the memory chip market, with consumer electronics, PC and smartphone makers racing to secure vital DRAM and NAND flash components for 2026, the report noted.

Several other components and materials related to chip substrates and printed circuit boards also face potential shortages this year. One example that some industry insiders pointed to is drill bits and drilling machines, used to drill layers of printed circuit boards for servers. In the past, one drill bit could be used many times . However, AI server boards have become thicker, harder and more expensive, which means drill bits must be much more advanced and replaced more frequently, the report added.

Recently, Nittobo told Nikkei Asia that it would continue to prioritize quality over quantity, and not become a maker of commoditized components.

"It's inevitable that we'll lose some market share," said Nittobo CEO Hiroyuki Tada. While he acknowledges the pressure to expand capacity, he says there is a limit to the risks that a small supplier like his can take on, the report noted.

Chris Lin, chairman and president of Aspeed Technology, a top developer of chips for server board management controllers, said constraints in chip substrates and other supply chain bottlenecks have held back some AI demand potential for 2026.

"We do see very strong demand and good growth momentum for 2026 but we do have to adjust down our forecast because of the constraints in material supplies," said Lin, the report noted.

data.title: ImmunityBio rises after Saudi approval of Anktiva for bladder cancer
data.description:
ImmunityBio (IBRX) shares added ~12% in the premarket on Wednesday after the Saudi Food and Drug Authority approved its best-selling immunotherapy, Anktiva, for certain patients with bladder cancer.

According to the San Diego, California-based biotech, the SFDA’s approval allows the company to market Anktiva plus Bacillus Calmette-Guérin ((BCG)) in the kingdom for adults with BCG-unresponsive non-muscle invasive bladder cancer.

Concurrently, the company announced plans to establish a regional office in Saudi Arabia to support its marketing efforts for Anktiva in the Middle East and North Africa, where Biopharma Cigalah serves as its commercial and distribution partner for the region.

Additionally, ImmunityBio (IBRX) stated that enrollments in its QUILT-2.005 trial, designed to test BCG plus Anktiva versus BCG alone in BCG-naïve patients, have exceeded expectations. The company continues to expect QUILT-2.005’s enrollments to be completed in Q2, and it plans to submit a marketing application for the drug by the end of 2026.

Approved in multiple countries, including the U.S. and the U.K., Anktiva helped ImmunityBio (IBRX) to generate $74.4M in net product revenue during the first nine months of last year.

data.title: Few surprises expected in Q4 as consumer softness persists
data.description:
In the fourth quarter, results from the personal care and beverage sector are expected to be largely predictable, though companies are likely to issue cautious long-term guidance amid continued softness in U.S. consumer spending.

Within the beverage category, Citi Research leans more positive towards Celsius Holdings (CELH) and PepsiCo (PEP), while in personal care, Citi favors Estée Lauder (EL) given solid momentum in Asia and improving U.S. trends.

PepsiCo (PEP) is first out of the gate, reporting before the market open on February 3. Concurrent with the company’s announcement regarding its strategic priorities agreement with Elliott Management, PepsiCo (PEP) reiterated FY25 guidance and provided initial FY26 outlook. This leads Citi Research to anticipate in-line Q4 results with sequential improvement at PepsiCo Foods North America ((PFNA)) expected to be viewed positively by the market.

Margins, however, could be pressured by the impacts from aluminum tariffs and volume deleverage, especially in PFNA with Citi predicting 80 basis points of compression to gross margin for the parent company. By segment, Citi expects PFNA operating margin to decline 160 basis points on volume deleverage, while PepsiCo Beverages North America ((PBNA)) should see a 150-basis point margin expansion thanks to recent pricing actions and the cycling of easier comparable sales in the fourth quarter of 2024.

Citi expects a “noisy” fourth quarter for Celsius (CELH) given pressures on the company’s topline and margins from the Alani Nu distribution transition, higher tariff impacts, seasonal distributor cash management activities, and the ongoing integration of the Rockstar brand. However, the firm retains a positive short-term view of the stock in respect of accelerating trends and new flavors for Alani Nu into the summer of 2026.

Pressure on the company’s margins likely remained in Q4 due to promotions, higher scrap and freight from the Alani Nu distribution integration, and increased tariff pressure. However, the company said it expects gross margin to re-expand in the first quarter of 2026 as Alani Nu and Rockstar Energy get further integrated into the CELH system.

Within the HPC space, Estée Lauder (EL) continues to gain momentum from depressed quarters in travel retail and China and shows “executional improvements resulting in further market share gains having established momentum in recent quarters,” Citi says.

Additionally, Citi thinks there is “positive setup for fiscal second quarter 2026 and sees increasing evidence of improving category trends, which will lead to raised FY26 organic sales growth/EPS guidance,” which will ultimately lead to increased confidence in Estee Lauder’s sustainable recovery and further gains in the share price.

Citi Research maintains a Buy rating for Celsius Holdings (CELH) and PepsiCo (PEP) with a Hold rating for Estée Lauder (EL) with a 9% bump to its price target to $120.

data.title: Femasys secures Nasdaq extension to regain bid price compliance
data.description:
• Femasys (FEMY) announced on Wednesday that it received a Nasdaq notification, approving the company's request for an additional 180-calendar-day extension to regain compliance with the minimum closing bid price requirement of $1.00 per share.
  • The Company now has until July 13, 2026, to regain compliance, as set forth in Nasdaq Listing Rule.
  • Nasdaq's extension notice has no immediate effect on the continued listing status of the company's common stock on The Nasdaq Capital Market LLC under the symbol "FEMY". 
  • FEMY -0.81% premarket to $0.5809.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: KLA Corp. in focus as Bernstein starts with Outperform rating
data.description:
KLA Corp. (KLAC) was in focus on Wednesday as Bernstein started coverage on the semiconductor equipment maker with an Outperform rating and $1,700 price target.

Shares fell 0.7% in premarket trading.

“KLA is one of the highest quality companies within [the semiconductor capital equipment space] and is the dominant player in Process Control, a $14.3B subsector within the larger $112B global [wafer fab equipment] market in CY24,” Bernstein analyst Stacy Rasgon wrote in a note to clients. “Process Control is crucial for semiconductor manufacturing and has grown at an ~18% CAGR between 2019 and 2024, even faster than the overall Wafer Fabrication Equipment market at ~15% CAGR. We estimate that Process Control intensity will rise further with the sector likely continuing to outpace [wafer fab equipment] over the next five years, with an ~11% CAGR from CY24-29 (vs. ~8% for [wafer fab equipment]), driven by several structural tailwinds (HPC/AI using larger die, more HBM and advanced packaging content etc.). KLA should benefit from this as the sector’s #1 player by far, with >6x the scale of its next largest competitor in the space at ~57% overall market share which it has even expanded over the last several years. We estimate that share gains will likely continue due to a strong competitive position and growth-favorable mix within Process Control subsegments.”

Delving deeper, Rasgon said that process control is likely to be “more insulated” than other parts of the wafer fab equipment market due to its complexity, making it harder for Chinese competition to replicate. And given that KLA Corp.'s exposure to China is already factored into its 2026 outlook, these numbers are “somewhat” derisked, he explained.

“Beyond the structural, we also see potential upside to medium-term numbers given likely
acceleration in the company’s services business (as installed base growth has picked up
markedly recently) as well as potential if Intel (INTC) continues on their foundry push which will likely necessitate structurally higher process control tool investment levels from them as
they build out,” Rasgon continued. “And we see positive optionality over the longer term in mask inspection with the company likely entering the actinic inspection market towards the end of the decade, timing their product launch with the wider proliferation of High-NA EUV tools in high-volume manufacturing.”

Rasgon also noted that KLA Corp. has a “very experienced and disciplined” management team that has struck the right chord between going after organic and inorganic growth, while at the same time returning lots of cash to shareholders.

“Putting it all together, we estimate that KLA can grow revenue and EPS in the low double
and mid double digits, respectively, over the next several years,” Rasgon added.
